raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's legislation requires elections officials to mail every active registered voter a ballot for all future elections.",
    "California voters will continue to have access to vote-by-mail ballot tracking.",
    "The requirements implemented prior to the 2020 General Election contributed to a secure election during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Mailing ballots to every active registered voter boosted voter turnout in California to recent highs.",
    "The 2020 General Election in California was conducted successfully despite an unprecedented onslaught of misinformation.",
    "Assemblymember Berman's AB 860 allowed counties to begin processing returned vote-by-mail ballots earlier.",
    "AB 860 extended the deadline for the receipt of vote-by-mail ballots.",
    "AB 860 allowed voters to use remote accessible vote-by-mail systems.",
    "AB 2218, introduced by Berman in 2018, created the ballot tracking system used in the 2020 election.",
    "Approximately 15 million Californians voted in the 2020 General Election using a ballot that was mailed to them.",
    "More than 4.5 million Californians registered for ballot tracking in the 2020 General Election.",
    "If signed into law, California would become the sixth state to require active registered voters to be mailed a ballot before each election."
  ]
}
```
